source.note: Unit 7 grammar chart and answer-key examples from the Our World Level 4 Grammar Workbook, cross-checked against the Lesson Planner (planner.pdf pp. 238-241).

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Used to for past habits
grammarPoints.rule: Use used to + verb to describe something that was true or happened regularly in the past but isn't true or doesn't happen anymore.

chart.title: Workbook Chart: Used to for past habits

intro_examples.text: I used to have long hair.
intro_examples.jp: 私は昔、髪が長かったです。

intro_examples.text: I used to walk to school.
intro_examples.jp: 私は昔、学校まで歩いていました。
今は自転車に乗っています。

intro_examples.text: People used to travel across the sea in boats.
intro_examples.jp: 昔、人々は船で海を渡っていました。

intro_examples.text: We didn't use to have airplanes.
intro_examples.jp: 私たちは昔、飛行機を持っていませんでした。

rows.example: People used to travel across the sea in boats.
rows.jp: どの人称でも used to のあとは動詞の原形です。

rows.example: We didn't use to have airplanes.
rows.jp: 否定文は didn't use to（used ではなく use）を使います。

rows.example: Why did people use to read by candlelight?
rows.jp: 疑問文は did のあとに use to を使います。

rows.pattern: used to + verb, but now + present
rows.example: I used to walk to school, but now I ride my bike.
rows.jp: but now を使って、昔と今を比べることもできます。

chart.note_rule: Used to shows something that was true or happened regularly in the past but isn't true or doesn't happen anymore.
chart.note_exception: Used to is different from be used to.
chart.note_exception_detail: I used to do homework at night = I did this regularly in the past, but not anymore. I am used to doing homework at night = I do this regularly now, and it is not a problem for me.

table.title: Used to for past habits

cells: People used to travel across the sea in boats.
We didn't use to have airplanes.
Why did people use to read by candlelight?
They didn't use to have electricity.

qa.question: Where did you use to play?
qa.answer: I used to play in the park.

qa.question: Did you use to play?
qa.answer: Yes, I did. / No, I didn't.

notes: Used to is different from be used to.
I used to do homework at night = I did this regularly in the past, but not anymore.
I am used to doing homework at night = I do this regularly now. It is not a problem for me.

tab1_samples.text: People used to travel across the sea in boats.
tab1_samples.jp: 昔、人々は船で海を渡っていました。

tab1_samples.text: We didn't use to have airplanes.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは昔、飛行機を持っていませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: Why did people use to read by candlelight?
tab1_samples.jp: なぜ昔の人々はろうそくの明かりで本を読んでいたのですか。

tab1_samples.text: They didn't use to have electricity.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼らは昔、電気を持っていませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: Before the invention of cars, people used to ride horses in the city.
tab1_samples.jp: 車が発明される前、人々は街で馬に乗っていました。

tab1_samples.text: Before the invention of the plane, people didn't use to travel by air.
tab1_samples.jp: 飛行機が発明される前、人々は空を移動していませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: In the 1950s, people didn't use to make calls with a cell phone.
tab1_samples.jp: 1950年代、人々は携帯電話で電話をかけていませんでした。

tab1_samples.text: I used to walk home from school every day.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は毎日、学校から歩いて家に帰っていました。

tab1_samples.text: My sister used to dry the dishes.
tab1_samples.jp: 私の姉/妹は昔、お皿を拭いていました。

tab1_samples.text: People used to tell stories.
tab1_samples.jp: 昔、人々は物語を語っていました。

rules.title: Used to shows a past habit that isn't true now
rules.jp_title: used toは今は違う過去の習慣を表す
rules.subtitle: We use used to to contrast a past situation with how things are now.
rules.jp_subtitle: used toを使って、過去の状況と今の様子を比べます。

transforms.to: I used to walk to school. Now I ride my bike.

transforms.from: People go to the movies more often these days.
transforms.to: People used to go to the movies more often. These days, they watch them at home.

examples.text: I used to have long hair, but now I have short hair.
examples.jp: 私は昔、髪が長かったですが、今は短いです。

examples.text: People used to write letters. These days, they send e-mails.
examples.jp: 昔、人々は手紙を書いていました。今はEメールを送ります。

rules.title: The negative is didn't use to, not didn't used to
rules.jp_title: 否定文は didn't use to（used ではない）
rules.subtitle: Used to loses the -d when it comes after didn't.
rules.jp_subtitle: didn't のあとの used to は -d がとれて use to になります。

transforms.from: We didn't used to have electricity.
transforms.to: We didn't use to have electricity.

transforms.from: They didn't used to have airplanes.
transforms.to: They didn't use to have airplanes.

examples.text: People didn't use to have cell phones.
examples.jp: 昔、人々は携帯電話を持っていませんでした。

examples.text: We didn't use to recycle very much.
examples.jp: 私たちは昔、あまりリサイクルをしていませんでした。

rules.title: The verb after used to is the same for every person
rules.jp_title: used toのあとの動詞はどの人称でも同じ形
rules.subtitle: Use used to + the base form of the verb for all persons: I/You/She/He/We/They used to walk to school.
rules.jp_subtitle: used to のあとはいつも動詞の原形を使います。

transforms.from: She used to walking to school.
transforms.to: She used to walk to school.

transforms.from: He used to walks to school.
transforms.to: He used to walk to school.

examples.text: My grandparents used to have a black and white TV.
examples.jp: 私の祖父母は昔、白黒テレビを持っていました。

examples.text: Children used to play outside more often.
examples.jp: 子どもたちは昔、もっと外で遊んでいました。

rules.title: Use did + use to for questions
rules.jp_title: 疑問文は did + use to
rules.subtitle: To form questions, use did + use to: What did people use to do in the past?
rules.jp_subtitle: 疑問文を作るときは did のあとに use to を置きます。

transforms.from: What people used to do in the past?
transforms.to: What did people use to do in the past?

transforms.from: You used to read by candlelight?
transforms.to: Did you use to read by candlelight?

examples.text: What did you use to play before video games?
examples.jp: テレビゲームの前は何をして遊んでいましたか。

examples.text: Did children use to play outside more?
examples.jp: 子どもたちは昔、もっと外で遊んでいましたか。

mixed_samples.text: People used to write letters instead of e-mails.
mixed_samples.jp: 昔、人々はEメールの代わりに手紙を書いていました。

mixed_samples.text: My grandparents used to have a black and white TV.
mixed_samples.jp: 私の祖父母は昔、白黒テレビを持っていました。

mixed_samples.text: People didn't use to have cell phones.
mixed_samples.jp: 昔、人々は携帯電話を持っていませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: We didn't use to recycle very much.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちは昔、あまりリサイクルをしていませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: What did people use to do for fun before TV?
mixed_samples.jp: テレビの前、人々は楽しみに何をしていましたか。

mixed_samples.text: How did people use to travel before planes?
mixed_samples.jp: 飛行機の前、人々はどうやって移動していましたか。

mixed_samples.text: What did children use to play with before video games?
mixed_samples.jp: テレビゲームの前、子どもたちは何で遊んでいましたか。

mixed_samples.text: I used to walk to school with my sister.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は姉/妹と一緒に学校まで歩いていました。

mixed_samples.text: I didn't use to wear glasses.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は昔、めがねをかけていませんでした。

mixed_samples.text: I used to have long hair, but now I have short hair.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は昔、髪が長かったですが、今は短いです。

stem: travel across the sea in boats.

explanation.title: Affirmative form
explanation.body: Use used to + the base verb for a past habit.

tab3_quiz.jp: 過去の習慣なので used to + 動詞の原形を使います。

explanation.title: Negative form
explanation.body: The negative is didn't use to — used loses the -d after didn't.

tab3_quiz.jp: 否定文は didn't use to で、used の -d はとれます。

stem: people use to read by candlelight?

explanation.title: Question form
explanation.body: Questions use did + use to, even for past habits.

tab3_quiz.jp: 疑問文は did のあとに use to を置きます。

stem: Before the invention of cars, people used to

explanation.title: Base verb after used to
explanation.body: The verb after used to is always the base form.

tab3_quiz.jp: used to のあとは動詞の原形（ride）を使います。

stem: make calls with a cell phone.

explanation.title: Past negative
explanation.body: Didn't use to describes something that wasn't a past habit.

tab3_quiz.jp: 過去の否定なので didn't use to を使います。

stem: I used to have long hair.

explanation.title: Contrast with now
explanation.body: Now shows the present is different from the past habit.

tab3_quiz.jp: Now は過去との違いを表すときに使います。

stem: have a black and white TV.

explanation.title: Every person, same form
explanation.body: Used to stays the same for every subject: I/you/he/she/we/they used to.

tab3_quiz.jp: used to はどの主語でも同じ形です。

stem: I used to do homework at night. Now I
doing homework at night, and it isn't a problem.

explanation.title: Used to vs. be used to
explanation.body: Am used to doing means it's a regular habit now, not the past.

tab3_quiz.jp: am used to doing は「今の習慣」を表し、used to（過去の習慣）とは違います。

explanation.title: Question needs use to
explanation.body: After did, use the base form use to, not used to.

tab3_quiz.jp: did のあとは use to（used ではない）を使います。

stem: Did children use to play outside

explanation.title: Comparing past and present
explanation.body: More compares how often children played outside in the past.

tab3_quiz.jp: more は「昔はもっと〜していた」を表します。

explanation.title: Past habit
explanation.body: Used to + base verb describes a repeated past action.

tab4_master.jp: 繰り返された過去の行動には used to + 動詞の原形を使います。

stem: have a bike when he was six.

explanation.title: Past negative
explanation.body: Didn't use to is the correct negative form.

tab4_master.jp: 正しい否定形は didn't use to です。

stem: your grandparents use to write letters?

explanation.title: Past question
explanation.body: Did starts a used to question about the past.

tab4_master.jp: 過去の質問は Did で始めます。

explanation.title: Base verb
explanation.body: Tell is the base form, correct after used to.

tab4_master.jp: used to のあとは tell（原形）を使います。

stem: I used to be scared of the dark.

explanation.title: Now for contrast
explanation.body: Now signals the present is different from the past.

tab4_master.jp: Now は「今は違う」ことを表します。

stem: have a computer at home until 2010.

explanation.title: Negative past habit
explanation.body: This describes something that wasn't true in the past — use didn't use to.

tab4_master.jp: 昔なかったことなので didn't use to を使います。

stem: up early now. It isn't hard for me anymore.

explanation.title: Be used to + -ing
explanation.body: Be used to is followed by the -ing form, and means a current habit — different from used to.

tab4_master.jp: be used to のあとは -ing 形で、今の習慣を表します。

explanation.title: Did + use to
explanation.body: After did, use the base form use to.

tab4_master.jp: did のあとは use to を使います。

tab4_master.cue: 私たちは昔、飛行機を持っていませんでした。
tab4_master.jp: 私たちは昔、飛行機を持っていませんでした。

tab4_master.cue: なぜ昔の人々はろうそくの明かりで本を読んでいたのですか。
tab4_master.jp: なぜ昔の人々はろうそくの明かりで本を読んでいたのですか。

japanese.title: used toで過去の習慣を表す
japanese.rule: used to + 動詞で、今はもう本当ではない、または起きなくなった過去の習慣や状態を表します。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + used to + 動詞

examples.sentence: People used to travel across the sea in boats.
We didn't use to have airplanes.
Why did people use to read by candlelight?
They didn't use to have electricity.
Before the invention of cars, people used to ride horses in the city.
Before the invention of the plane, people didn't use to travel by air.
In the 1950s, people didn't use to make calls with a cell phone.
I used to walk home from school every day.
My sister used to dry the dishes.
People used to tell stories.
People used to write letters instead of e-mails.
My grandparents used to have a black and white TV.
People didn't use to have cell phones.
We didn't use to recycle very much.
What did people use to do for fun before TV?
How did people use to travel before planes?
What did children use to play with before video games?
I used to walk to school with my sister.
I didn't use to wear glasses.
I used to have long hair, but now I have short hair.

workbookActivities.instruction: Circle the correct answer: used to or use to.
Complete the conversations with did, use to, or didn't use to.
Write five sentences about when you were younger. Write two that aren't true.
Complete the sentences about Jamie using used to or didn't use to.
Complete the chart like Jamie's. What used to be different for you? Choose categories and write sentences.

source.note: Grammar chart and exercises sourced from Level 4 Grammar Workbook answer key, Unit 7, and the Our World Level 4 Lesson Planner (planner.pdf pp. 238-241).

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: You for General Statements
grammarPoints.rule: Use you to talk about people in general, not one specific person.

chart.title: Workbook Chart: You for general statements

intro_examples.text: You need to have creativity to invent things.
intro_examples.jp: 発明するには創造力が必要です。

intro_examples.text: You should always try again if you fail.
intro_examples.jp: 失敗しても、いつも再び挑戦するべきです。

intro_examples.text: What do you do with this invention?
intro_examples.jp: この発明品を何に使いますか。

rows.pattern: You + verb (true for people in general)
rows.jp: 人々一般について話すとき you を使います。

rows.example: You need to work hard to succeed.
rows.jp: should・need to・have to・must などのあとに you を続けます。
What や How のあとに do you を使って、一般的な使い方をたずねます。

rows.pattern: you = the one person being spoken to
rows.example: Can you help me fix my bike?
rows.jp: you が「話している相手」一人だけを指すこともあります。

chart.note_rule: You can mean people in general, not one specific person.
chart.note_exception: You can also mean just the one person you're talking to.
chart.note_exception_detail: Compare: You should recycle plastic. (people in general) vs. Can you help me fix my bike? (one specific person — the listener).

cells: What do you do on this computer?
You can play games and do homework.
How do you make vegetable soup?
First, you wash the vegetables. Then you . . .

tab1_samples.text: You need to have creativity to invent things.
tab1_samples.jp: 発明するには創造力が必要です。

tab1_samples.text: You should always try again if you fail.
tab1_samples.jp: 失敗しても、いつも再び挑戦するべきです。

tab1_samples.text: You should recycle plastic.
tab1_samples.jp: プラスチックはリサイクルするべきです。

tab1_samples.text: You must wear protective clothes.
tab1_samples.jp: 防護服を着なければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: You don't have to use scissors.
tab1_samples.jp: はさみを使う必要はありません。

tab1_samples.text: You can't feed the animals at the zoo.
tab1_samples.jp: 動物園で動物にえさをあげてはいけません。

tab1_samples.text: You need to drink water.
tab1_samples.jp: 水を飲む必要があります。

tab1_samples.text: You should get a new scooter.
tab1_samples.jp: 新しいスクーターを買うべきです。

tab1_samples.text: You use it to tell time.
tab1_samples.jp: それは時間を知るために使います。

tab1_samples.text: You use it to carry books to school.
tab1_samples.jp: それは学校に本を運ぶために使います。

rules.title: You means people in general, not one person
rules.jp_title: you は特定の一人ではなく人々一般を表す
rules.subtitle: We use you to make a statement that is true for people in general, not just one person.
rules.jp_subtitle: you を使って、一人だけでなく人々一般に当てはまることを言います。

examples.jp: プラスチックはリサイクルするべきです。
防護服を着なければなりません。

rules.title: Modal + you for advice and rules
rules.jp_title: should・need to・have to などのあとに you
rules.subtitle: Use modals like should, need to, have to, and must before the verb to give general advice or rules.
rules.jp_subtitle: should・need to・have to・must などのあとに動詞を続けて、一般的な助言やルールを言います。

transforms.from: You have creativity to invent things.
transforms.to: You need to have creativity to invent things.

transforms.from: You try again if you fail.
transforms.to: You should always try again if you fail.

examples.text: You need to have creativity to invent things.
examples.jp: 発明するには創造力が必要です。

examples.text: You don't have to use scissors.
examples.jp: はさみを使う必要はありません。

rules.title: How/What do you ...? asks about general instructions
rules.jp_title: How/What do you ...? で一般的な使い方をたずねる
rules.subtitle: Use What/How do you + verb to ask how something is generally used or done.
rules.jp_subtitle: What/How do you のあとに動詞を続けて、一般的なやり方をたずねます。

transforms.from: You do what with this invention?
transforms.to: What do you do with this invention?

examples.jp: それをどうやって使いますか。

examples.text: What do you do with this invention?
examples.jp: この発明品を何に使いますか。

rules.title: You can also mean one specific person
rules.jp_title: you が特定の一人を指すこともある
rules.subtitle: In context, you can mean just the person you're talking to, not people in general.
rules.jp_subtitle: 文脈によっては、you は人々一般ではなく、話している相手一人だけを指すこともあります。

transforms.to: Can you help me fix my bike?
You should get a new scooter.

examples.text: Can you help me fix my bike?
examples.jp: 自転車を直すのを手伝ってくれますか。

examples.text: You should get a new scooter.
examples.jp: 新しいスクーターを買うべきです。

mixed_samples.text: You should recycle plastic.
mixed_samples.jp: プラスチックはリサイクルするべきです。

mixed_samples.text: You must wear protective clothes.
mixed_samples.jp: 防護服を着なければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: You need to have creativity to invent things.
mixed_samples.jp: 発明するには創造力が必要です。

mixed_samples.text: You don't have to use scissors.
mixed_samples.jp: はさみを使う必要はありません。

mixed_samples.text: How do you use it?
mixed_samples.jp: それをどうやって使いますか。

mixed_samples.text: What do you do with this invention?
mixed_samples.jp: この発明品を何に使いますか。

mixed_samples.text: First, you wash the vegetables. Then you cook them.
mixed_samples.jp: まず野菜を洗います。それから調理します。

mixed_samples.text: You can play games and do homework on this computer.
mixed_samples.jp: このコンピューターでゲームをしたり宿題をしたりできます。

mixed_samples.text: Can you help me fix my bike?
mixed_samples.jp: 自転車を直すのを手伝ってくれますか。

mixed_samples.text: You should get a new scooter.
mixed_samples.jp: 新しいスクーターを買うべきです。

explanation.title: General you
explanation.body: You talks about people in general here, not one specific person.

tab3_quiz.jp: 人々一般について話しているので you を使います。

explanation.title: Modal + base verb
explanation.body: Must is followed directly by the base form of the verb.

tab3_quiz.jp: must のあとは動詞の原形をそのまま続けます。

explanation.title: Don't have to
explanation.body: You is followed by don't have to, not has to, for a general rule.

tab3_quiz.jp: you のあとは don't have to を使います。

explanation.title: Asking how something is used
explanation.body: How do you ...? asks about the general way something is used.

tab3_quiz.jp: How do you ...? で一般的な使い方をたずねます。

explanation.title: Do after do you
explanation.body: After do you, use the base form of the verb: do.

tab3_quiz.jp: do you のあとは動詞の原形 do を使います。

explanation.title: General instructions use you
explanation.body: General instructions (like a recipe) use you for anyone following the steps.

tab3_quiz.jp: レシピなど一般的な手順の説明では you を使います。

stem: Which sentence is about people in general, not one person?
answers: Can you help me fix my bike?
Are you coming to my house?

explanation.title: General vs. specific you
explanation.body: "You should recycle plastic" is a rule for everyone, not a question to one listener.

tab3_quiz.jp: 「You should recycle plastic.」は特定の相手ではなく、みんなに当てはまるルールです。

stem: Which sentence is about ONE specific person?
answers: Can you help me fix my bike?

explanation.title: Specific you
explanation.body: "Can you help me fix my bike?" is asking the one listener for help, not making a general rule.

tab3_quiz.jp: 「Can you help me fix my bike?」は話している相手一人に頼んでいます。

explanation.title: To + verb for purpose
explanation.body: Use to + the base verb to explain what something is used for.

tab3_quiz.jp: 何のために使うかを言うときは to + 動詞の原形を使います。

stem: always try again if you fail.

explanation.title: Should + base verb
explanation.body: Should is followed directly by the base form of the verb, with no to.

tab3_quiz.jp: should のあとは to をつけず、動詞の原形をそのまま続けます。

stem: must wear protective clothes at the factory.

explanation.title: General you
explanation.body: You is the subject pronoun used for people in general.

tab4_master.jp: 人々一般を表す主語は you です。

explanation.title: Need to + verb
explanation.body: Need is followed by to + the base verb.

tab4_master.jp: need のあとは to + 動詞の原形を使います。

explanation.title: Have to + base verb
explanation.body: Have to (and don't have to) is followed by the base form of the verb.

tab4_master.jp: have to のあとは動詞の原形を使います。

explanation.title: How for a process
explanation.body: How do you ...? asks about the general steps for doing something.

tab4_master.jp: How do you ...? で一般的なやり方をたずねます。

explanation.title: To + verb for purpose
explanation.body: To + the base verb explains the purpose of something.

tab4_master.jp: 目的を言うときは to + 動詞の原形を使います。

explanation.title: Can't + base verb
explanation.body: Can't is followed directly by the base form of the verb.

tab4_master.jp: can't のあとは動詞の原形をそのまま続けます。

stem: you help me fix my bike?

explanation.title: Can you ...? for a request
explanation.body: Can you ...? asks the one listener for help — this you is specific, not general.

tab4_master.jp: Can you ...? は話している相手一人に頼む表現です。

explanation.title: Then for the next step
explanation.body: Then signals the next step in a set of general instructions.

tab4_master.jp: Then は手順の「次のステップ」を表します。

tab4_master.cue: 防護服を着なければなりません。
tab4_master.jp: 防護服を着なければなりません。

tab4_master.cue: それをどうやって使いますか。
tab4_master.jp: それをどうやって使いますか。

japanese.title: you で人々一般について話す
japanese.rule: you を使って、特定の一人ではなく人々一般について話すことができます。
japanese.pattern: you + 動詞（一般的な内容）

examples.sentence: You need to have creativity to invent things.
You should always try again if you fail.
You should recycle plastic.
You must wear protective clothes.
You don't have to use scissors.
You can't feed the animals at the zoo.
You need to drink water.
You should get a new scooter.
You use it to tell time.
You use it to carry books to school.
How do you use it?
What do you do with this invention?
First, you wash the vegetables. Then you cook them.
You can play games and do homework on this computer.
Can you help me fix my bike?
You need to have creativity to invent things.
You don't have to use scissors.
How do you use it?
What do you do with this invention?
Can you help me fix my bike?

workbookActivities.instruction: Rewrite the sentences. Use you instead of the underlined words.
Unscramble. Circle the number of sentences that are NOT about people in general.
Look and write. What do you use each item for? Write a sentence with you.
Draw an invention. Answer the questions: What is it? When/How/Why do you use it?
Choose an object from the box. Explain how, when, and why you use it.

source.note: Grammar chart and exercises sourced from Level 4 Grammar Workbook answer key, Unit 7 (page 9 of the PDF), and the Our World Level 4 Lesson Planner (planner.pdf pp. 244-245).
